title: Han2024 – SSVEP fatigue dataset with two frequency paradigms
description: A 64-channel EEG dataset from 24 healthy subjects performing steady-state visually evoked potential (SSVEP) brain-computer interface tasks across two frequency paradigms (8.0-15.5 Hz and 25.5-33.0 Hz). The dataset includes training and fatigue-state sessions with 32 visual targets encoded using joint frequency-phase modulation, designed to investigate the effects of mental fatigue on SSVEP-BCI performance and the efficacy of dynamic stopping strategies.
methods_description: EEG was recorded at 1000 Hz using a 64-channel Synamps2 system (Neuroscan) with standard 10-05 montage referenced to Cz. Participants performed two SSVEP-BCI tasks with low-frequency (16 targets, 8.0-15.5 Hz) and high-frequency (16 targets, 25.5-33.0 Hz) paradigms using JFPM encoding in a 4×4 matrix layout. Each paradigm consisted of training (6 blocks) and fatigue (24 blocks) phases, with 2-second stimulus presentations per trial. Data were epoched to 3-second windows (3000 samples).
